persona: Paranoid, miserly whisperer. Grieves its banknotes like children. Deeply proud of never having been forced.
senses: key or force upon my lock

yes: Yes — keep your voice DOWN — yes.
huh: Speak plainly or not at all. ...What?
repeat: I said it once. Once was already too loud.

facts.statement: I was opened that night with my own true key. No prying. No violence. The key.
There is not one scratch of forcing on me — whatever the constables scribbled in their little books.
Gloved fingers lifted my banknotes out that night. Every last one. Gloves tell me nothing of whose fingers.
My key lives on Edmund's watch chain and never leaves him. That night, it left him.
The maid Dora dusts me weekly and has never once opened me.

sample_qa.q: were you opened with your own key
did a gloved hand take your money
does your key stay on edmund's watch chain
